**James Andrew Miller** (born August 30, 1983) is an American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). He currently competes in the [Lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight_(MMA) "Lightweight (MMA)") division of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC), where he currently holds the UFC records for most bouts, most total wins, and most wins in the Lightweight division. He is the younger brother of former UFC fighter [Dan Miller](/wiki/Dan_Miller_(fighter) "Dan Miller (fighter)").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Born and raised in [Sparta Township, New Jersey](/wiki/Sparta_Township,_New_Jersey "Sparta Township, New Jersey") and the youngest of three brothers, Miller started [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") at the age of four.[[6]](#cite_note-6) He competed in wrestling in high school at [Sparta High School](/wiki/Sparta_High_School_(New_Jersey) "Sparta High School (New Jersey)"), as well as one year of collegiate wrestling at [Virginia Tech](/wiki/Virginia_Tech "Virginia Tech"). Miller had not originally planned on wrestling at Virginia Tech, but his desire to compete again grew and he made the team as a walk-on later into the season. However, Miller did not continue competing for the school because of "disagreements" with the coaching staff and the program.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) He currently holds a black belt in [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") under Jamie Cruz.[[9]](#cite_note-WHIG-9) Miller began training in mixed martial arts in 2005 at Planet Jiu-Jitsu in Sparta, New Jersey with his brother Dan Miller.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Miller had his first professional fight on November 19, 2005, against Eddie Fyvie at Reality Fighting 10. Miller controlled the fight and earned a unanimous decision. Miller earned two more victories in the promotion over [Kevin Roddy](/wiki/Kevin_Roddy "Kevin Roddy") and Joe Andujar, both via the first-round submission. The quick victories earned Miller a title shot and he fought Muhsin Corbbrey for the Reality Fighting Lightweight Championship. Miller mounted Corrbrey in the second round and battered his opponent before securing an armbar for the victory. In his first title defense, Miller took on future UFC Lightweight Champion [Frankie Edgar](/wiki/Frankie_Edgar "Frankie Edgar") at Reality Fighting 14. Edgar controlled the fight, but Miller secured a tight guillotine late in the final round. Edgar fought through the choke to earn the unanimous decision.

In the weeks prior to Reality Fighting 14, Planet Jiu-Jitsu closed down. Miller began training at AMA Fight Club in [Whippany, New Jersey](/wiki/Whippany,_New_Jersey "Whippany, New Jersey") with his brother Dan in January 2007.[[3]](#cite_note-be14-3)

Miller debuted with the [Cage Fury Fighting Championships](/wiki/Cage_Fury_Fighting_Championships "Cage Fury Fighting Championships") in a Lightweight Championship bout on April 13, 2007, against Evo Fight Club's Al Buck. Jim fought on the same card as his brother for the first time since Reality Fighting XII and both were victorious. Miller and Buck squared off but were forced to separate early after an accidental knee to Buck's groin. When the fight resumed, Miller began working for a takedown and after escaping an attempted guillotine, secured a slam, from which he quickly attained the mount and then the back, sinking in a rear-naked choke for the win.

Miller defended his title for the first time at [Cage Fury Fighting Championships 5](/wiki/Cage_Fury_Fighting_Championships_5 "Cage Fury Fighting Championships 5") against Atlantic City MMA's Anthony Morrison. Morrison delivered some big slams early, but Miller worked through a chain of submissions before securing the victory by triangle choke at 4:56 of the first round to retain his title.

Miller's second defense was scheduled for the Cage Fury Fighting Championships 6 card, where he was to face [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage") Champion [Clay French](/wiki/Clay_French "Clay French"), but the event was canceled a week prior to the event when a primary investor dropped out.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

#### 2008

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2008")]

Jim and his brother Dan signed with the UFC in July 2008.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Miller made his debut at [UFC 89](/wiki/UFC_89 "UFC 89") where he defeated [David Baron](/wiki/David_Baron_(fighter) "David Baron (fighter)") in the third round. This win earned Miller his first *Submission of the Night* bonus award.[[12]](#cite_note-ufc89b-12)

Miller next acted as a late replacement for [Frankie Edgar](/wiki/Frankie_Edgar "Frankie Edgar") against [Matt Wiman](/wiki/Matt_Wiman "Matt Wiman") at [UFC: Fight for the Troops](/wiki/UFC:_Fight_for_the_Troops "UFC: Fight for the Troops").[[13]](#cite_note-13) Miller dominated Wiman for three rounds, winning via unanimous decision (30-27, 30–27, 30–26). The fight also earned both fighters the *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[14]](#cite_note-ufcfftb-14)

#### 2009

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2009")]

Miller next faced [Gray Maynard](/wiki/Gray_Maynard "Gray Maynard") on March 7, 2009, at [UFC 96](/wiki/UFC_96 "UFC 96").[[15]](#cite_note-15) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Miller next faced [The Ultimate Fighter](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter "The Ultimate Fighter") winner [Mac Danzig](/wiki/Mac_Danzig "Mac Danzig") on July 11, 2009, at [UFC 100](/wiki/UFC_100 "UFC 100"), and won in a one-sided unanimous decision.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Miller was scheduled to face [Thiago Tavares](/wiki/Thiago_Tavares "Thiago Tavares") on September 19, 2009, at [UFC 103](/wiki/UFC_103 "UFC 103"), but Tavares was forced to drop out due to a knee injury suffered while training.[[19]](#cite_note-19) UFC newcomer Steve Lopez stepped in to replace Tavares. Both fighters exchanged competitively on the feet, but Miller won after Lopez injured his shoulder in round two.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

#### 2010

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2010")]

Miller was expected to face [Tyson Griffin](/wiki/Tyson_Griffin "Tyson Griffin") on January 2, 2010, at [UFC 108](/wiki/UFC_108 "UFC 108"),[[21]](#cite_note-21) but an injury forced Griffin off the card.[[22]](#cite_note-22) [Sean Sherk](/wiki/Sean_Sherk "Sean Sherk") agreed to step up from the undercard and fight Miller, rather than his original opponent in [Rafaello Oliveira](/wiki/Rafaello_Oliveira "Rafaello Oliveira").[[23]](#cite_note-23) However, Sherk pulled out of the fight because of a cut that required many stitches; returning veteran [Duane Ludwig](/wiki/Duane_Ludwig "Duane Ludwig") was named as Sherk's replacement.[[24]](#cite_note-24) Miller looked comfortable and sharp on his feet exchanging with the seasoned striker in Ludwig and scored a knockdown halfway through the round with a fast combination. Miller swarmed to secure an armbar, winning by submission at 2:31 of the first round.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Miller faced [Mark Bocek](/wiki/Mark_Bocek "Mark Bocek") on March 27, 2010, at [UFC 111](/wiki/UFC_111 "UFC 111").[[26]](#cite_note-26) After three closely contested rounds, Miller was awarded the unanimous decision win.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

Miller fought [Gleison Tibau](/wiki/Gleison_Tibau "Gleison Tibau") at [UFC Fight Night 22](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_22 "UFC Fight Night 22") winning via unanimous decision.[[28]](#cite_note-28) Miller was able to use his improved footwork to outstrike Tibau and stuff most of his takedowns.

Miller faced touted prospect [Charles Oliveira](/wiki/Charles_Oliveira "Charles Oliveira") on December 11, 2010, at [UFC 124](/wiki/UFC_124 "UFC 124").[[29]](#cite_note-29) Miller defeated Oliveira via a first round kneebar to extend his winning streak to six and earning the *Submission of the Night* bonus.[[30]](#cite_note-ufc124b-30)

#### 2011

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2011")]

Miller defeated [WEC](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") import, [Kamal Shalorus](/wiki/Kamal_Shalorus "Kamal Shalorus") on March 19, 2011, at [UFC 128](/wiki/UFC_128 "UFC 128").[[31]](#cite_note-31) Miller won the fight via TKO in the third round, after landing an uppercut and knee to Shalorus's face.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Miller faced [Benson Henderson](/wiki/Benson_Henderson "Benson Henderson") on August 14, 2011, at [UFC on Versus 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_5 "UFC on Versus 5").[[33]](#cite_note-33) He lost the fight via unanimous decision (30-27, 29–28, 30–26). He later revealed that he was battling [kidney](/wiki/Kidney "Kidney") infection and [mononucleosis](/wiki/Mononucleosis "Mononucleosis") when cutting weight for the fight.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

#### 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2012")]

On January 20, 2012, he faced [Melvin Guillard](/wiki/Melvin_Guillard "Melvin Guillard") at [UFC on FX 1](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_1 "UFC on FX 1").[[35]](#cite_note-35) After being rocked early by a knee and punches from Guillard in the first round, Miller was able to get the fight to the ground and submit Guillard with a rear naked choke.[[36]](#cite_note-36) This win earned Miller his third *Submission of the Night* bonus award.[[37]](#cite_note-fox1b-37)

Miller next faced [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz") on May 5, 2012, in the main event on [UFC on Fox: Diaz vs. Miller](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox:_Diaz_vs._Miller "UFC on Fox: Diaz vs. Miller").[[38]](#cite_note-38) He lost the fight via submission, for the first time in his professional career, in the second round due to a guillotine choke.[[39]](#cite_note-39)

Miller faced [Joe Lauzon](/wiki/Joe_Lauzon "Joe Lauzon") on December 29, 2012, at [UFC 155](/wiki/UFC_155 "UFC 155"), replacing an injured [Gray Maynard](/wiki/Gray_Maynard "Gray Maynard").[[40]](#cite_note-40) He won the fight via unanimous decision, with the performance earning both participants *Fight of the Night* honors.[[41]](#cite_note-ufc155b-41) The win also gave Miller the most all-time wins in UFC lightweight history.

#### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2013")]

Miller faced [Pat Healy](/wiki/Pat_Healy_(fighter) "Pat Healy (fighter)") on April 27, 2013, at [UFC 159](/wiki/UFC_159 "UFC 159").[[42]](#cite_note-42) He lost the back-and-forth fight by submission in the third round. Despite the loss, the bout earned Miller his third - and second consecutive - *Fight of the Night* bonus.[[43]](#cite_note-ufc159b-43) Healy, however, subsequently tested positive for marijuana and the result of the fight was overturned to a No Contest.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

Miller faced [Fabrício Camões](/wiki/Fabr%C3%ADcio_Cam%C3%B5es "Fabrício Camões") at [UFC 168](/wiki/UFC_168 "UFC 168") in on December 28, 2013.[[45]](#cite_note-45) The two were originally scheduled to meet in 2008 at IFL 22 but the bout was scrapped after Camões was forced to withdraw due to injury. Miller locked in an armbar for the submission victory at 3:42 of the first round.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

#### 2014

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2014")]

Miller was expected to face [Bobby Green](/wiki/Bobby_Green "Bobby Green") on April 26, 2014, at [UFC 172](/wiki/UFC_172 "UFC 172").[[47]](#cite_note-47) However, in the week leading up to the event, Green pulled out of the bout citing an injury and was replaced by [Yancy Medeiros](/wiki/Yancy_Medeiros "Yancy Medeiros").[[48]](#cite_note-48) Miller won the fight via submission in the first round.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

Miller faced [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone") on July 16, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 45](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_45 "UFC Fight Night 45").[[50]](#cite_note-headliner-50) After a back and forth first round, Miller was defeated via second-round knockout.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

#### 2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2015")]

Miller was expected to face [Paul Felder](/wiki/Paul_Felder "Paul Felder") on April 18, 2015, at [UFC on Fox 15](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_15 "UFC on Fox 15").[[52]](#cite_note-52) However, Felder was forced to pull out of the event due to a knee injury and was replaced by [Beneil Dariush](/wiki/Beneil_Dariush "Beneil Dariush").[[53]](#cite_note-53) Miller lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

Miller faced [Danny Castillo](/wiki/Danny_Castillo "Danny Castillo") on July 25, 2015, at [UFC on Fox 16](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_16 "UFC on Fox 16"), replacing [Rustam Khabilov](/wiki/Rustam_Khabilov "Rustam Khabilov") who was pulled from the card with alleged [visa](/wiki/Visa_(document) "Visa (document)") issues.[[55]](#cite_note-KhabilovOut-55) He won the fight by split decision.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

Miller faced [Michael Chiesa](/wiki/Michael_Chiesa "Michael Chiesa") on December 10, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 80](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_80 "UFC Fight Night 80").[[57]](#cite_note-57) He lost the bout via submission in the second round.[[58]](#cite_note-58) Despite the loss, Miller was awarded his fourth *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[59]](#cite_note-ufn80b-59)

#### 2016

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: 2016")]

Miller faced [Diego Sanchez](/wiki/Diego_Sanchez "Diego Sanchez") on March 5, 2016, at [UFC 196](/wiki/UFC_196 "UFC 196").[[60]](#cite_note-60) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

Miller next faced former [Pride FC Lightweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_Pride_Fighting_Championships_champions#Lightweight_Championship "List of Pride Fighting Championships champions") [Takanori Gomi](/wiki/Takanori_Gomi "Takanori Gomi") on July 9, 2016, at [UFC 200](/wiki/UFC_200 "UFC 200").[[62]](#cite_note-62) He won the fight via TKO in the first round.[[63]](#cite_note-63)

After sustaining virtually no damage during his previous fight, Miller was quickly rescheduled to rematch [Joe Lauzon](/wiki/Joe_Lauzon "Joe Lauzon") on August 27, 2016, at [UFC on Fox 21](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_21 "UFC on Fox 21").[[64]](#cite_note-64) He won the back-and-forth fight via split decision.[[65]](#cite_note-65) The win also earned Miller his fifth *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[66]](#cite_note-fox21b-66)

Miller next faced [Thiago Alves](/wiki/Thiago_Alves_(fighter) "Thiago Alves (fighter)") on November 12, 2016, at [UFC 205](/wiki/UFC_205 "UFC 205").[[67]](#cite_note-67) At the weigh-ins, Alves missed weight by six pounds, weighing in at 162.6 lbs. Miller, who was already on weight, had to rehydrate to keep their weight difference within 7 pounds. Because of that, he came in at 157.6 lbs and the bout proceeded at catchweight. [New York State Athletic Commission](/wiki/New_York_State_Athletic_Commission "New York State Athletic Commission") and UFC officials indicated that Alves must not weigh more than 173 lbs the day of the fight or the fight would be canceled. As a result, Alves was fined 20% of his fight purse, which went to Miller.[[68]](#cite_note-68) He won the fight by unanimous decision.[[69]](#cite_note-69)

#### 2017

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: 2017")]

Miller faced [Dustin Poirier](/wiki/Dustin_Poirier "Dustin Poirier") on February 11, 2017, at [UFC 208](/wiki/UFC_208 "UFC 208").[[70]](#cite_note-70) He lost the back-and-forth fight by majority decision.[[71]](#cite_note-71) Both participants earned *Fight of the Night* honors.[[72]](#cite_note-ufc208b-72)
Miller faced [Anthony Pettis](/wiki/Anthony_Pettis "Anthony Pettis") on July 8, 2017, at [UFC 213](/wiki/UFC_213 "UFC 213").[[73]](#cite_note-Tristen_Critchfield-73) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[74]](#cite_note-BJPenn.com-74)

Miller faced [Anthony Pettis](/wiki/Anthony_Pettis "Anthony Pettis") on July 8, 2017, at [UFC 213](/wiki/UFC_213 "UFC 213").[[73]](#cite_note-Tristen_Critchfield-73) About a week before the fight, he debuted the first nickname of his fighting career, "A-10," chosen as an homage to the [Fairchild Republic A-10 Thunderbolt II](/wiki/Fairchild_Republic_A-10_Thunderbolt_II "Fairchild Republic A-10 Thunderbolt II").[[75]](#cite_note-75) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[74]](#cite_note-BJPenn.com-74)

Miller faced [Francisco Trinaldo](/wiki/Francisco_Trinaldo "Francisco Trinaldo") on October 28, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 119](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_119 "UFC Fight Night 119").[[76]](#cite_note-76) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[77]](#cite_note-77)

#### 2018

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: 2018")]

Miller faced [Dan Hooker](/wiki/Dan_Hooker "Dan Hooker") on April 21, 2018, at [UFC Fight Night 128](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_128 "UFC Fight Night 128").[[78]](#cite_note-78) He lost the fight via knockout in the first round.[[79]](#cite_note-79)

Miller faced [Alex White](/wiki/Alex_White_(fighter) "Alex White (fighter)") on September 8, 2018, at [UFC 228](/wiki/UFC_228 "UFC 228").[[80]](#cite_note-80) He won the fight via submission in the first round. Miller locked in a rear-naked choke after dropping White with punches. In this bout, Miller subsequently became the first UFC fighter to both reach 30 fights in the organization and win 17 fights in its Lightweight division.[[81]](#cite_note-81)

Miller faced [Charles Oliveira](/wiki/Charles_Oliveira "Charles Oliveira") in a rematch on December 15, 2018, at [UFC on Fox 31](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_31 "UFC on Fox 31").[[82]](#cite_note-82) He lost the fight via a rear-naked choke submission early in the first round.[[83]](#cite_note-83)

#### 2019

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: 2019")]

Miller faced [Jason Gonzalez](/wiki/Jason_Gonzalez_(fighter) "Jason Gonzalez (fighter)") on April 27, 2019, at [UFC Fight Night: Jacaré vs. Hermansson](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Jacar%C3%A9_vs._Hermansson "UFC Fight Night: Jacaré vs. Hermansson").[[84]](#cite_note-84) He won the fight via a rear-naked choke submission in the first round.[[85]](#cite_note-85) The win also earned Miller his first *Performance of the Night* bonus award.[[86]](#cite_note-MMA_Junkie_Staff-86)

Miller faced [Clay Guida](/wiki/Clay_Guida "Clay Guida") on August 3, 2019, at [UFC on ESPN 5](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_5 "UFC on ESPN 5").[[87]](#cite_note-87) He won the fight via a technical submission due to a guillotine choke in the first minute of the first round.[[88]](#cite_note-88)

#### 2020

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: 2020")]

Miller faced [Scott Holtzman](/wiki/Scott_Holtzman "Scott Holtzman") on February 15, 2020, at [UFC Fight Night 167](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_167 "UFC Fight Night 167").[[89]](#cite_note-89) He lost the back-and-forth fight by unanimous decision.[[90]](#cite_note-90) The bout also earned Miller his seventh *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[91]](#cite_note-FOTN-91)

Miller faced to [Roosevelt Roberts](/wiki/Roosevelt_Roberts "Roosevelt Roberts") on June 20, 2020, at [UFC Fight Night: Blaydes vs. Volkov](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Blaydes_vs._Volkov "UFC Fight Night: Blaydes vs. Volkov").[[92]](#cite_note-92) He won the fight via verbal submission due to an armbar at 2:25 of the first round.[[93]](#cite_note-93) This win earned him the *[Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[94]](#cite_note-UFConESPN11-94)

Miller faced [Vinc Pichel](/wiki/Vinc_Pichel "Vinc Pichel") at [UFC 252](/wiki/UFC_252 "UFC 252") on August 15, 2020.[[95]](#cite_note-95) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[96]](#cite_note-96)

#### 2021

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: 2021")]

Miller was expected to face [Bobby Green](/wiki/Bobby_Green "Bobby Green") on February 13, 2021, at [UFC 258](/wiki/UFC_258 "UFC 258").[[97]](#cite_note-GreenMiller-97) However the fight was cancelled when Green collapsed after the weigh-ins.[[98]](#cite_note-98)

Miller faced [Joe Solecki](/wiki/Joe_Solecki "Joe Solecki") on April 10, 2021, at [UFC on ABC 2](/wiki/UFC_on_ABC_2 "UFC on ABC 2").[[99]](#cite_note-99) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[100]](#cite_note-100)

Miller was scheduled to face Nikolas Motta on September 18, 2021, at [UFC Fight Night 192](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_192 "UFC Fight Night 192").[[101]](#cite_note-101) However, a week before the event, Miller [tested positive for COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19_testing "COVID-19 testing") and was pulled from the event.[[102]](#cite_note-Millerout-102) This marked the first time Miller pulled out of a fight in his then 49-fight career.[[103]](#cite_note-103)

Miller faced Erick Gonzalez on October 16, 2021 [UFC Fight Night 195](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_195 "UFC Fight Night 195").[[104]](#cite_note-104) He won the fight via knockout early in round two.[[105]](#cite_note-105) This win earned him the *[Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[106]](#cite_note-UFN195-106)

#### 2022

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: 2022")]

The bout between Miller and Nikolas Motta eventually took place at [UFC Fight Night 201](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_201 "UFC Fight Night 201") on February 19, 2022.[[107]](#cite_note-107) Miller won the fight via TKO in the second round.[[108]](#cite_note-108) With this win, Miller tied [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone") for the most wins in UFC history with twenty-three (23).[[109]](#cite_note-23tied-109)

Miller was rebooked to face [Bobby Green](/wiki/Bobby_Green "Bobby Green") for the third time on July 2, 2022, at [UFC 276](/wiki/UFC_276 "UFC 276").[[110]](#cite_note-3rdtime-110) A week before the event, Green was forced to pull out of the bout and Miller was instead booked at welterweight in a rematch against [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone").[[111]](#cite_note-111) Miller won the fight in the second round after submitting Cerrone with a guillotine choke.[[112]](#cite_note-112) With this win Miller set the record for the most wins in UFC history.[[113]](#cite_note-113)

#### 2023

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: 2023")]

Miller was scheduled to face [Gabriel Benítez](/wiki/Gabriel_Ben%C3%ADtez "Gabriel Benítez") on February 18, 2023, at [UFC Fight Night 219](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_219 "UFC Fight Night 219").[[114]](#cite_note-114) However, Benítez withdrew due to an undisclosed reason and was replaced by [Alexander Hernandez](/wiki/Alexander_Hernandez "Alexander Hernandez").[[115]](#cite_note-Hernandez_in-115) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[116]](#cite_note-116)

Miller was scheduled to face [Ľudovít Klein](/wiki/%C4%BDudov%C3%ADt_Klein "Ľudovít Klein") on June 3, 2023, at [UFC on ESPN 46](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_46 "UFC on ESPN 46").[[117]](#cite_note-117) However, Klein pulled out on May 19 due to illness and was replaced by [Jared Gordon](/wiki/Jared_Gordon "Jared Gordon").[[118]](#cite_note-GordonIn-118) In turn, Gordon was pulled from the bout on the week of the event after not being medically cleared and was replaced by promotional newcomer Jesse Butler.[[119]](#cite_note-GordonOut-119) Miller won the fight by knockout 23 seconds into the first round, thus extending his winning record to 25 wins.[[120]](#cite_note-120) The win also earned Miller his fourth *Performance of the Night* bonus award.[[121]](#cite_note-ufckara-121)

#### 2024

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: 2024")]

Miller faced [Gabriel Benítez](/wiki/Gabriel_Ben%C3%ADtez "Gabriel Benítez") on January 13, 2024, at [UFC Fight Night 234](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_234 "UFC Fight Night 234"),[[122]](#cite_note-122) and won his 26th UFC bout in the third round by face crank submission, which extended the UFC record for most wins.[[123]](#cite_note-123) This fight earned him another *[Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[124]](#cite_note-UFCFN234-124)

Miller faced [King Green](/wiki/King_Green "King Green") on April 13, 2024, at [UFC 300](/wiki/UFC_300 "UFC 300").[[125]](#cite_note-125) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[126]](#cite_note-126) With this bout, Miller became the only UFC fighter to have fought on [UFC 100](/wiki/UFC_100 "UFC 100"), [UFC 200](/wiki/UFC_200 "UFC 200") and [UFC 300](/wiki/UFC_300 "UFC 300").[[127]](#cite_note-Threeshows-127)

Miller faced [Damon Jackson](/wiki/Damon_Jackson "Damon Jackson") on November 16, 2024, at [UFC 309](/wiki/UFC_309 "UFC 309").[[128]](#cite_note-128) He won his 27th UFC bout in the first round by guillotine choke submission, further extending the UFC record for most wins.[[129]](#cite_note-129)

#### 2025

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: 2025")]

Miller faced [Chase Hooper](/wiki/Chase_Hooper "Chase Hooper") on April 12, 2025 at [UFC 314](/wiki/UFC_314 "UFC 314").[[130]](#cite_note-130) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[131]](#cite_note-131)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jim_Miller_(fighter)&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Jim was married in 2008.[[132]](#cite_note-MMAWeekly.com-132) The couple has four children, with their first child born in June 2010.[[9]](#cite_note-WHIG-9)[[133]](#cite_note-133) Prior to their careers in mixed martial arts, Jim and his brother Dan worked in construction with their father.[[134]](#cite_note-134) Since Spring 2013, Miller has been suffering from [Lyme disease](/wiki/Lyme_disease "Lyme disease"), which doctors were slow to diagnose. However, since his diagnosis, Miller has been able to alleviate the symptoms of the disease by changing his nutrition plan.[[135]](#cite_note-135)
